description: Nintendo and Game Freak's monster-catching RPG franchise. This library prioritizes ROM hacks and definitive editions over originals — several vanilla games are intentionally omitted in favour of superior versions.

media.title: Pokémon Crystal Clear
media.description: Open-world Gold/Silver/Crystal with level scaling, full Pokédex access, and a customizable protagonist. Replaces Gold, Silver, and Crystal.
media.notes: Core: gambatte. ROM hack — patch a clean Crystal ROM. Save ID: crystal-clear.

media.title: Pokémon Ultra Violet
media.description: FireRed with all 386 Pokémon obtainable in a single save, no trade evolutions, and bug fixes. The definitive Kanto experience. Replaces Red, Blue, Yellow, FireRed, and LeafGreen.
media.notes: Core: mgba. ROM hack — patch a clean FireRed ROM. Save ID: ultra-violet. Step 1 of recommended play order.

media.title: Pokémon Emerald Rogue
media.description: Roguelite Hoenn — each run is a randomly generated adventure with infinite replayability. Replaces Ruby, Sapphire, and Emerald for replay purposes.
media.notes: Core: mgba. ROM hack — patch a clean Emerald ROM. Save ID: emerald-rogue.

media.title: Pokémon Unbound
media.description: Fully custom region with Gen 8 mechanics, difficulty modes, and a polished story. The ultimate fan-game experience on GBA hardware.
media.notes: Core: mgba. ROM hack — patch a clean FireRed ROM. Save ID: unbound. Step 5 of recommended play order.

media.title: Pokémon HeartGold
media.description: Johto + Kanto across two full regions. Pokémon follow you on the overworld. The most beloved official remake — Gen 1 through 4 Pokédex.
media.notes: Core: melonds. Save ID: heartgold. Step 2 of recommended play order.

media.title: Pokémon SoulSilver
media.description: Identical to HeartGold in content — version exclusives differ. Play one or both.
media.notes: Core: melonds. Save ID: soulsilver.

media.title: Pokémon Black
media.description: Unova region. Best story in the mainline series — Team Plasma questions whether Pokémon training is ethical. Gen 5 Pokémon only until post-game.
media.notes: Core: melonds. Save ID: black. Step 3 of recommended play order.

media.title: Pokémon White
media.description: Version counterpart to Black — different version exclusives and the White Forest vs Black City area.
media.notes: Core: melonds. Save ID: white.

media.title: Pokémon Black 2
media.description: Direct sequel set 2 years after Black/White — the only direct sequels in the franchise. New areas, callbacks to Black/White, and improved post-game.
media.notes: Core: melonds. Save ID: black2. Play Black first.

media.title: Pokémon White 2
media.description: Version counterpart to Black 2. Same sequel content with version exclusives.
media.notes: Core: melonds. Save ID: white2.

media.title: Renegade Platinum
media.description: Platinum with all 493 Pokémon obtainable, no trade evolutions, faster engine, and a significantly higher difficulty curve. Replaces Diamond, Pearl, and Platinum.
media.notes: Core: melonds. ROM hack — patch a clean Platinum ROM. Save ID: renegade-platinum. Step 4 of recommended play order.

media.title: Pokémon X
media.description: First 3D mainline Pokémon. Kalos region. Introduced Mega Evolutions and the Fairy type.
media.notes: 3DS — requires Citra/Lime3DS/Azahar. Not browser-playable. Sunshine streaming needed.

media.title: Pokémon Y
media.description: Version counterpart to X — different Mega Evolutions and version exclusives.
media.notes: 3DS — requires Citra/Lime3DS/Azahar. Not browser-playable. Sunshine streaming needed.

media.title: Pokémon Omega Ruby / Alpha Sapphire
media.description: 3DS remakes of Ruby and Sapphire with expanded post-game, Mega Evolutions, and the Delta Episode story content.
media.notes: 3DS — requires Citra/Lime3DS/Azahar. Two ROMs (OR and AS).

media.title: Pokémon Ultra Sun / Ultra Moon
media.description: Expanded versions of Sun/Moon — the definitive Alola experience. Replaces Sun and Moon entirely.
media.notes: 3DS — requires Citra/Lime3DS/Azahar. Two ROMs (US and UM).

media.title: Pokémon Sword
media.description: Galar region. Introduced the Wild Area open-world zone and Dynamax. Controversial for reduced Pokédex but plays well.
media.notes: Switch — requires Yuzu/Ryujinx. Not browser-playable. Sunshine streaming needed.

media.title: Pokémon Shield
media.description: Version counterpart to Sword — different version exclusives and a few gym differences.
media.notes: Switch — requires Yuzu/Ryujinx. Not browser-playable. Sunshine streaming needed.

media.title: Pokémon Legends: Arceus
media.description: Action-RPG set in feudal Sinnoh. Radically different from mainline games — catching in real time without battling. Best entry in years.
media.notes: Switch — requires Yuzu/Ryujinx. Not browser-playable. Sunshine streaming needed.

media.title: Pokémon Scarlet
media.description: Paldea region. First fully open-world mainline Pokémon with three parallel story paths. Paradox Pokémon are ancient forms.
media.notes: Switch — requires Yuzu/Ryujinx. Not browser-playable. Sunshine streaming needed.

media.title: Pokémon Violet
media.description: Version counterpart to Scarlet — future Paradox Pokémon instead of ancient. Same open-world structure.
media.notes: Switch — requires Yuzu/Ryujinx. Not browser-playable. Sunshine streaming needed.

media.title: Pokémon: Indigo League
media.description: Ash and Pikachu begin their journey across Kanto. The original series — Ash catches his first team, rivals Gary, and enters the Pokémon League. Runs parallel to Red/Blue/Yellow.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Concurrent with Gen 1 games. Start here for the classic experience.

media.title: Pokémon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
media.description: Ash completes a filler arc through the anime-exclusive Orange Islands archipelago, picking up Lapras and competing in the Orange League. Misty and Brock temporarily replaced by Tracey.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Filler arc between Kanto and Johto — can be lightly skimmed.

media.title: Pokémon: Johto Journeys
media.description: Ash, Misty, and Brock arrive in the Johto region. First season of the Gold/Silver arc — early Johto gyms and new Pokémon introductions. Concurrent with Gold/Silver/Crystal.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 1 of 3 Johto seasons.

media.title: Pokémon: Johto League Champions
media.description: Ash continues through Johto, collecting gym badges and meeting more Gold/Silver Pokémon. Mid-season of the Johto arc.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 2 of 3 Johto seasons.

media.title: Pokémon: Master Quest
media.description: Ash finishes the Johto circuit and enters the Silver Conference. Concludes the Johto arc. Features Ho-Oh and Lugia prominently.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 3 of 3 Johto seasons. Concurrent with HeartGold/SoulSilver.

media.title: Pokémon: Advanced
media.description: Ash arrives in Hoenn. Brock rejoins; May and Max are introduced as new companions. First Ruby/Sapphire arc season. Pokémon Contests introduced.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 1 of 4 Hoenn/AG seasons.

media.title: Pokémon: Advanced Challenge
media.description: Ash continues through Hoenn gyms while May competes in Pokémon Contests. Team Magma and Team Aqua appear. Concurrent with Ruby/Sapphire/Emerald.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 2 of 4 Hoenn/AG seasons.

media.title: Pokémon: Advanced Battle
media.description: Ash reaches the Ever Grande Conference; May enters the Grand Festival. Groudon and Kyogre arc concludes. Strong season overall.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 3 of 4 Hoenn/AG seasons.

media.title: Pokémon: Battle Frontier
media.description: Ash returns to Kanto to tackle the Battle Frontier — seven high-difficulty facilities tied to Emerald's post-game. May heads to Johto. Scott and the Frontier Brains are the main antagonists.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 4 of 4 AG seasons. Concurrent with Emerald post-game.

media.title: Pokémon: Diamond and Pearl
media.description: Ash heads to Sinnoh with Dawn as new companion. Dawn's Piplup arc begins. Competitive Pokémon Contests evolve into Pokémon Contests vs Pokémon Battles dual storylines. Concurrent with Diamond/Pearl.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 1 of 4 Sinnoh/DP seasons.

media.title: Pokémon: Diamond and Pearl — Battle Dimension
media.description: Ash collects more Sinnoh badges while Team Galactic's plans begin to unfold. Dawn enters more Contests. Paul rivalry intensifies — one of the better rivalries in the franchise.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 2 of 4 Sinnoh/DP seasons.

media.title: Pokémon: Diamond and Pearl — Galactic Battles
media.description: Team Galactic's plan to summon Dialga/Palkia/Giratina reaches its climax. Some of the best villain writing in the anime. Ash vs Paul rivalry hits its peak. Concurrent with Platinum.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 3 of 4 Sinnoh/DP seasons.

media.title: Pokémon: Diamond and Pearl — Sinnoh League Victors
media.description: Ash enters the Lily of the Valley Conference. The Ash vs Paul battle is the best animated battle in the original series run. Dawn's arc concludes. Concurrent with Renegade Platinum.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 4 of 4 Sinnoh/DP seasons.

media.title: Pokémon: Black & White
media.description: Ash arrives in Unova with new companions Iris and Cilan. Continuity is soft-reset — Ash's Pokémon stay home, fresh start. Team Plasma looms in the background. Concurrent with Black/White.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 1 of 3 Unova/BW seasons.

media.title: Pokémon: Black & White — Rival Destinies
media.description: Ash finishes collecting Unova gym badges. N and Team Plasma storyline begins. Rival Trip becomes more prominent. Concurrent with Black 2/White 2 timeline.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 2 of 3 Unova/BW seasons.

media.title: Pokémon: Black & White — Adventures in Unova and Beyond
media.description: The Unova League and N/Team Plasma arc concludes. Post-league filler arc through the Decolore Islands wraps up the Unova saga.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 3 of 3 Unova/BW seasons.

media.title: Pokémon the Series: XY
media.description: Ash arrives in Kalos with new companions Serena, Clemont, and Bonnie. Series reboot in animation quality — noticeably more fluid and cinematic. Mega Evolution introduced. Concurrent with X/Y.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 1 of 3 Kalos/XY seasons.

media.title: Pokémon the Series: XY — Kalos Quest
media.description: Ash continues through Kalos gyms. Serena's Pokémon Showcase arc develops. Team Flare's plan begins to take shape. Concurrent with Omega Ruby/Alpha Sapphire.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 2 of 3 Kalos/XY seasons.

media.title: Pokémon the Series: XYZ
media.description: The strongest season of the Ash era. Zygarde's story unfolds, Team Flare launches their full attack on Lumiose City. Ash-Greninja transformation is a highlight. Best animation of the classic run. Fan-favourite season.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 3 of 3 Kalos/XY seasons. Highly recommended even as a standalone for lapsed fans.

media.title: Pokémon the Series: Sun & Moon
media.description: Ash moves to Alola and attends the Pokémon School. Major art style shift — more chibi and comedic. Z-Moves replace Mega Evolution. Divisive among fans but ends well. Concurrent with Sun/Moon.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 1 of 3 Alola/SM seasons.

media.title: Pokémon the Series: Sun & Moon — Ultra Adventures
media.description: Ultra Beasts are introduced through the Ultra Guardians storyline. Ash's Poipole/Naganadel arc is a fan highlight. Concurrent with Ultra Sun/Ultra Moon.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 2 of 3 Alola/SM seasons.

media.title: Pokémon the Series: Sun & Moon — Ultra Legends
media.description: The Alola League — Ash finally wins his first regional Pokémon League championship after 22 years of the anime. Major milestone. Necrozma arc climaxes here.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 3 of 3 Alola/SM seasons. Ash wins the League — a landmark episode.

media.title: Pokémon Journeys: The Series
media.description: Ash and new companion Goh travel across all regions entering the World Coronation Series tournament. Goh's Pokémon-catching quest mirrors the game's Pokédex completion. Concurrent with Sword/Shield.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 1 of 3 Journeys seasons.

media.title: Pokémon Master Journeys: The Series
media.description: Ash climbs the World Coronation Series rankings toward the Masters Eight. Chloe joins as third companion. Heavy fanservice — battles include previous rivals and legendary Pokémon.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 2 of 3 Journeys seasons.

media.title: Pokémon Ultimate Journeys: The Series
media.description: Ash challenges the Masters Eight and faces Leon for the World Championship. The climax of Ash's 25-year journey. Spectacular final battles. Concurrent with Legends: Arceus and Scarlet/Violet.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 3 of 3 Journeys seasons. Ash's final arc as protagonist.

media.title: Pokémon: To Be a Pokémon Master
media.description: A short farewell miniseries following Ash and Pikachu on one final journey revisiting old friends and companions before the torch is passed. Closure for the 25-year era.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Watch immediately after Ultimate Journeys.

media.title: Pokémon Horizons: The Series
media.description: New protagonists Liko and Roy join the Rising Volt Tacklers airship crew. Fresh start — no prior series knowledge needed. Murdock, Friede, and Captain Pikachu are the standout characters. Concurrent with Scarlet/Violet.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Standalone — ideal entry point if starting fresh. No Ash knowledge required.

media.title: Pokémon: The First Movie — Mewtwo Strikes Back
media.description: Mewtwo clones the world's Pokémon and challenges Ash. The iconic Gen 1 theatrical film. 'The fighting… it has to stop.' Watch after Indigo League.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Watch after Indigo League or anytime.

media.title: Pokémon: The Movie 2000 — The Power of One
media.description: Ash must collect three legendary birds and Lugia to save the world. Lugia's first major appearance. Watch during/after Orange Islands.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Watch during Orange Islands arc.

media.title: Pokémon 3: The Movie — Spell of the Unown
media.description: The Unown construct a crystal tower and kidnap Ash's mother. Entei plays a central role. Watch during Johto Journeys.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Watch during Johto arc.

media.title: Pokémon 4Ever — Celebi: Voice of the Forest
media.description: Ash and Sam (a boy from 40 years in the past) must protect Celebi from the Iron-Masked Marauder. Time travel plot. Watch during Johto League Champions.
media.notes: Jellyfin.

media.title: Pokémon Heroes — Latios and Latias
media.description: Set in the Venice-inspired Alto Mare city. Latios and Latias protect the city from thieves seeking the Soul Dew. Watch during the Battle Frontier or Johto wrap-up.
media.notes: Jellyfin.

media.title: Pokémon: Jirachi — Wish Maker
media.description: Ash and friends help Jirachi return to its comet before a corrupt magician can exploit its wish-granting power. Watch during Advanced Challenge.
media.notes: Jellyfin.

media.title: Pokémon: Destiny Deoxys
media.description: Deoxys and Rayquaza battle over a futuristic city. Set in the Hoenn era. Watch during Advanced Battle.
media.notes: Jellyfin.

media.title: Pokémon: Lucario and the Mystery of Mew
media.description: Ash and a time-displaced Lucario search for Mew to save the Tree of Life. One of the highest-regarded Pokémon films. Watch during Battle Frontier.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Fan-favourite movie.

media.title: Pokémon Ranger and the Temple of the Sea
media.description: Ash helps protect Manaphy and the Sea Temple from pirates. Watch during Diamond and Pearl.
media.notes: Jellyfin.

media.title: Pokémon: The Rise of Darkrai
media.description: Darkrai defends Alamos Town from a space-time rift caused by battling Dialga and Palkia. Part 1 of the Sinnoh movie trilogy. Watch during DP Battle Dimension.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 1 of Sinnoh movie trilogy.

media.title: Pokémon: Giratina and the Sky Warrior
media.description: Giratina and Shaymin are caught in a battle over the Reverse World. Part 2 of the Sinnoh movie trilogy. Watch during DP Galactic Battles.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 2 of Sinnoh movie trilogy.

media.title: Pokémon: Arceus and the Jewel of Life
media.description: Arceus awakens in a rage and threatens the world. Time travel resolves the ancient betrayal at the heart of the conflict. Part 3 and conclusion of the Sinnoh movie trilogy.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Part 3 of Sinnoh movie trilogy — watch after The Rise of Darkrai and Giratina.

media.title: Pokémon: Zoroark — Master of Illusions
media.description: Zoroark is forced to create illusions for a corrupt businessman while searching for her captured child Zorua. First Gen 5 movie, watch during Black & White.
media.notes: Jellyfin.

media.title: Pokémon the Movie: Black — Victini and Reshiram
media.description: One of two alternate versions of the same story — Ash teams with Reshiram (Black version) to restore Victini's freedom. Watch during BW Rival Destinies.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Two alternate cuts exist (Black/Reshiram and White/Zekrom) — content is near-identical.

media.title: Pokémon the Movie: White — Victini and Zekrom
media.description: Alternate version of the Victini film — Ash allies with Zekrom instead of Reshiram. Same story, different Pokémon framing.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Watch one or both — Black/Reshiram version is the more commonly recommended cut.

media.title: Pokémon the Movie: Kyurem vs. the Sword of Justice
media.description: Keldeo challenges Kyurem to prove its worth as a Sword of Justice. Watch during BW Adventures in Unova.
media.notes: Jellyfin.

media.title: Pokémon the Movie: Genesect and the Legend Awakened
media.description: A team of ancient Genesect invades New Tork City and clashes with Mewtwo (a new incarnation). Final Unova-era film. Watch during XY.
media.notes: Jellyfin.

media.title: Pokémon the Movie: Diancie and the Cocoon of Destruction
media.description: Diancie seeks Xerneas to create a new Heart Diamond for her kingdom while thieves pursue her. First Kalos movie. Watch during XY Kalos Quest.
media.notes: Jellyfin.

media.title: Pokémon the Movie: Hoopa and the Clash of Ages
media.description: Hoopa's imprisoned shadow form battles a cast of legendary Pokémon summoned through dimensional rings. Watch during XYZ.
media.notes: Jellyfin.

media.title: Pokémon the Movie: Volcanion and the Mechanical Marvel
media.description: Ash is bound to a reluctant Volcanion and must help it protect Magearna from a corrupt minister. Final Kalos movie. Watch during Sun & Moon early seasons.
media.notes: Jellyfin.

media.title: Pokémon the Movie: I Choose You!
media.description: Retelling/reimagining of Ash and Pikachu's origin story with new characters replacing Misty and Brock. Marshadow plays a key role. Alternate timeline — does not connect to main series continuity.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Alternate universe retelling — not a sequel to the original Indigo League.

media.title: Pokémon the Movie: The Power of Us
media.description: An ensemble of townspeople must work together to save Fula City during the annual Wind Festival. Ash appears but is a supporting character. Zeraora features heavily.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Standalone — alternate universe continuation of I Choose You.

media.title: Pokémon: Mewtwo Strikes Back — Evolution
media.description: CGI remake of the original First Movie. Near shot-for-shot with updated visuals. Watch the 1998 original instead unless you specifically want the CGI version.
media.notes: Jellyfin. CGI remake — the 1998 original is the recommended version.

media.title: Pokémon: Secrets of the Jungle
media.description: Ash meets Koko, a boy raised by Zarude in an ancient jungle. Environmental themes. Part of the Journeys-era continuity.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Watch during Pokémon Journeys.

media.title: Pokémon: Detective Pikachu
media.description: Live-action film. Tim Goodman teams up with a talking Pikachu to find his missing father in Ryme City. Standalone — no anime knowledge required.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Standalone — no anime knowledge required.

media.title: Pokémon Origins
media.description: Four-episode mini-series following Red (not Ash) through a faithful retelling of the original Red/Blue/FireRed story. Intended as a more serious, game-accurate companion to the main anime. Mewtwo and Mega Charizard X featured. Fan-favourite for its tone and fidelity.
media.notes: Jellyfin. 4 episodes — watch after or alongside Ultra Violet. Completely standalone.

media.title: Pokémon Generations
media.description: 18 short web episodes (3–5 minutes each) dramatizing key moments from each game generation — the takedown of Team Rocket, Ghetsis's arrest, Lysandre's final monologue, and more. Covers Gen 1 through Gen 6. Released weekly on the official Pokémon YouTube channel.
media.notes: Jellyfin / YouTube. Each episode is a self-contained scene. Watch after completing the corresponding game generation.

media.title: Pokémon: Twilight Wings
media.description: Seven short episodes plus a feature-length finale set in the Galar region during the events of Sword/Shield. Follows various human characters — a sick boy, Leon's rival Hop, gym challengers — rather than a traditional protagonist. High production quality from Studio Colorido.
media.notes: Jellyfin / YouTube. Watch during or after Pokémon Sword/Shield. 8 episodes including the extended finale.

media.title: Pokémon: Hisuian Snow
media.description: Three-episode web series set in ancient Hisui (feudal Sinnoh) from Pokémon Legends: Arceus. A young man returns to Hisui and recounts his father's story of befriending a Zorua. Beautiful Wit Studio animation. Released on the official Pokémon YouTube channel.
media.notes: Jellyfin / YouTube. 3 short episodes. Watch during or after Legends: Arceus.

media.title: Pokémon Concierge
media.description: Stop-motion animated Netflix series. Haru starts a new job as a concierge at a resort exclusively for Pokémon. Soft, slice-of-life tone — a complete tonal departure from the main anime. Charming and short.
media.notes: Jellyfin / Netflix. 4 episodes. Fully standalone — no prior series knowledge needed.

media.title: Pokémon Adventures (Pokémon Special)
media.description: The definitive Pokémon manga — 62+ volumes running since 1997, covering every main game generation with its own protagonist (Red, Gold, Ruby, Diamond, Black, X, Sun, Sword…). Darker, more complex, and more faithful to the games than the anime. Widely considered the best non-game Pokémon media. Each story arc is self-contained and follows the corresponding game's plot with significant original additions. Written by Hidenori Kusaka, art by Mato (Gens 1–2) and Satoshi Yamamoto (Gen 3+).
media.notes: Physical / digital. Arcs map 1:1 to game generations — read the RGB arc with Gen 1, the GSC arc with Gen 2, etc. VIZ Media publishes in English. Ongoing.

media.title: Pokémon Zensho
media.description: Two-volume manga by Satoshi Yamamoto (before he took over Adventures art duties) following Shigeru (Blue's counterpart) through a condensed Kanto journey. The first Pokémon manga, predating Adventures. More grounded and fast-paced than the anime. Licensed in Japan only — fan translations available.
media.notes: Fan translation only — not officially released outside Japan. 2 volumes.

media.title: Pokémon: The Electric Tale of Pikachu
media.description: Four-volume manga by Toshihiro Ono loosely adapting the anime's early Kanto arc. Follows Ash but with a more mature art style and several deviations from the show. One of the few Pokémon manga to receive a full English localization in the original Western Pokémon boom.
media.notes: VIZ Media English release (out of print — secondary market). 4 volumes.

media.title: Pokémon: Magical Journey
media.description: Shojo manga following a girl named Pichu who can communicate with Pokémon. Lighthearted and targeted at younger readers. Less well-known outside Japan.
media.notes: Limited English release. 3 volumes.

media.title: Pokémon: I Choose You! (novelization)
media.description: Novelization of the original anime's early Kanto arc by Takeshi Shudō (the anime's original head writer). Provides additional backstory and Shudō's own expanded lore — including his controversial vision for where the series was heading. Considered essential reading for anime lore historians.
media.notes: Japanese only — no official English release. Fan translation available.

media.title: Pokémon: The First Movie novelization
media.description: Junior novelization of Mewtwo Strikes Back by Tracey West. Targeted at young readers — a quick companion read to the film. Part of the Scholastic Pokémon chapter book line.
media.notes: Scholastic English release. Collectible — widely available secondhand.
